sections.paragraph:
and עֲרֹעֵר (from the root עָרַר; like קְלֹוקֵל (from the root קָלַל; تلاتل from the root تلّ)

(1) probab. i.q. עַרְעָר prop. naked; hence needy, outcast, Jer 48:6 (compare Jer 17:6). LXX. ὄνος ἄγριος (עָרֹור); Vulgate myrica (compare Arab. عَرْعَرُ juniper, Wachholder).

(2) [Aroer], pr.n.-(a) of a town on the northern bank of the river Arnon, Deu 2:36 3:12 4:48 Jos 12:2 13:16 which belonged to Moab, Jer 48:19 another form is עַרְעֹור Jud. 11:26 . Its ruins still bear the ancient name (عرعاير); see Burckhardt’s Travels in Syria, p. 633. Different from this is-(b) another farther north, over against Rabbath Ammon ( Jos 13:25), situated on the river of Gad; i.e. an arm of Jabbok, 2Sa 24:5 built by the Gadites, Num 32:34 Isa 17:2 (see my observations on the passage).-(c) a town of the tribe of Judah, 1Sa 30:28. Gent. n. עֲרֹעֵרִי 1Ch 11:44.
